name: Heroes of the Pacific
post_apply_message:
Heroes of the Pacific (2005, non-Steam) — 1920x1200 widescreen fix + auto-shortcut. The game ignores VideoQuery.exe's resolution and forces 800x600; the AlphaYellow ASI patches that.
Apply installs: (1) HeroesOfThePacificWidescreenFix.asi + its ini (Width=1920 Height=1200 FOVFactor=1.0); (2) ThirteenAG's Ultimate ASI Loader dropped in as winmm.dll (this game hooks via winmm); (3) the Steam shortcut for Heroes.exe (gospel appid 4105667313 — your existing prefix) with WINEDLLOVERRIDES="winmm=n,b" so Wine loads the native winmm loader, on GE-Proton 11-1 (Tony confirmed this game needs 11-1, not 10.34).
Stage GE first with 'gfm stage-runner'. Then just launch — it should come up at 1920x1200 instead of 800x600. If it's still 800x600, the winmm loader didn't hook: confirm the launch option is on the shortcut, and that Heroes.exe (not HOTP.exe) is the exe that renders.

HOW TO TELL IF THE FIX ACTUALLY WORKED: set the Deck/Legion scaling to FIT (not Stretch). 1920x1200 IS the Legion's native 16:10, so a working fix fills the panel at Fit with no stretching. If you need Stretch to fill, or you see a 4:3 pillarboxed box, the ASI is NOT hooking and the game is still rendering 800x600 upscaled — which looks soft/blurry and is easily mistaken for missing AA.

EXPECTED EVEN WHEN WORKING: (1) the game has NO anti-aliasing option at all (PCGW), so it stays jaggy — that's the game, not the fix; (2) it is HARD-CAPPED AT 30 FPS with no known unlock patch — RenderWare/Direct3D 8 console port, the cap is baked in. Don't chase either.

CONTROLLER: the game has NATIVE Xbox 360 controller support (in-game Options > Controls), so no mod is needed. On the Deck/Legion, turn Steam Input ON for this shortcut with a Gamepad layout and enable 'Xbox Configuration Support' — Steam Input already presents the pad as a 360 controller, which HOTP accepts directly. If it doesn't detect the pad, x360ce (drop its xinput dlls next to Heroes.exe) is the fallback, same as Driver SF — not bundled yet, ask if you want it added.
notes:
Built 2026-07-10. Retail/abandonware (readmes are the original multi-language ones, not a Chemical crack). The game forces 800x600 regardless of VideoQuery.exe -> AlphaYellow Widescreen & FOV Fix (github alphayellow1/AlphaYellowWidescreenFixes, release tag heroesofthepacific, v1.1 rar) committed here (payload/widescreen, .asi 1.3MB + ini set to 1920x1200) since the download is a rar. Loader = ThirteenAG Ultimate ASI Loader v9.7.2 (same pinned zip as barnyard, single universal dinput8.dll) fetched + dropped in as winmm.dll via copy_files 'as' (new option). WINEDLLOVERRIDES winmm=n,b loads it. Exe = Heroes.exe (6.7MB; per Tony, vs HOTP.exe 3.8MB / VideoQuery.exe config). Prefix already exists at appid 4105667313 = the gospel (no drift).

PCGW facts confirmed 2026-07-13: engine = RenderWare, API = Direct3D 8 (32-bit) — note D3D8, not 9. FPS is CAPPED AT 30 with no unlock listed (Tony asked; answer is no fps patch exists). NO anti-aliasing option in-game (workarounds only) — so jaggies are expected and are NOT a sign the widescreen fix failed; the real tell is whether it fills a 16:10 panel at Fit scaling. Config lives in the REGISTRY (HKLM\SOFTWARE\Codemasters\Heroes Of The Pacific) so a prefix backup covers it. SAVES are at <game>/save — LOWERCASE per PCGW; save_paths lists both cases because Linux is case-sensitive and the on-disk casing of Tony's copy is unconfirmed (the wrong one just logs 'nothing there, skipped'). Retail DRM was SecuROM 7.12. RUNNER: Tony confirmed on real hardware 2026-07-13 that this one needs GE-Proton11-1 (recipe originally pinned GE-Proton10-34) — install_runner + steam_shortcut both bumped. GE-Proton11-1.tar.gz is staged on the NAS _runners/ (pristine upstream copy, sha512-verified).
